phrases.text_local: せんぼんとりいは どこからですか？
phrases.cultural_note: The iconic torii gates begin immediately behind the main hall and are very obvious.

likely_response.local: ほんでんの うしろです。すぐ わかります。
likely_response.translation_en: Behind the main shrine. You'll see it right away.

responses.english: Behind the main shrine. You'll see it right away.
responses.french: ほんでんの うしろです。すぐ わかります。

phrases.text_local: おまもりは どこですか？
phrases.cultural_note: Omamori are usually sold at a dedicated window or building (社務所 or 授与所) near the main hall.

likely_response.local: ほんでんの よこです。

responses.french: ほんでんの よこです。

phrases.text_local: やまみちは きょう いけますか？
phrases.cultural_note: The trails on Mt. Inari are always open and free. A polite warning to be careful is common.

likely_response.local: はい、いけます。きをつけて。
likely_response.translation_en: Yes, you can go. Be careful.

responses.english: Yes, you can go. Be careful.
responses.french: はい、いけます。きをつけて。

phrases.text_local: おみくじは どこで ひけますか？
phrases.cultural_note: Omikuji are often found near the shrine office or omamori counter, sometimes in a box where you shake a stick.

likely_response.local: しゃむしょの まえです。ひだりがわに あります。
likely_response.translation_en: In front of the shrine office. It's on the left side.

responses.english: In front of the shrine office. It's on the left side.
responses.french: しゃむしょの まえです。ひだりがわに あります。
